LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS INT TECHNOLOGIES LLC, ITECH US INC., MERIDIAN TECHNOLOGIES INC., PALNAR, RELIANCE GLOBAL SERVICES, INC., S4 CONSULTANTS INC., SPARATAN SOLUTIONS AND MEGANET SOLUTIONS LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE THEIR PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT MAKES NO DETERMINATION AS TO WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE PERMISSIVE OR COMPULSORY BECAUSE DEFENDANTS WOULD BE GRANTED LEAVE UNDER EITHER STANDARD. PURSUANT TO CCP 426.50, A COURT MAY ONLY DENY LEAVE TO FILE A COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT IF IT FINDS THE DEFENDANT IS ACTING IN BAD FAITH. (SILVER ORGANIZATIONS LTD. V. FRANK (1990) 217 CAL.APP.3D 94, 98-99.) NO SHOWING OF BAD FAITH HAS BEEN MADE HERE. IF THE CLAIMS ARE PERMISSIVE, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SUBSTANTIALLY SIMILAR CLAIMS OF CAMPBELL & SPANGLER AND DEFENDANTS SHOULD BE TRIED IN THE SAME ACTION. NO TRIAL DATE HAS YET BEEN SET. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Ro Cross-Complaints By All Cross Complainants
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. HAROLD P. SMITH of Smith, LLP, Tel No. (510) 418-4942 appearing for the Cross-Complainants INT Technologies, LLC; iTech US, Inc.; Meganet Solutions, LLC; Meridian Technologies, Inc.; PALNAR; Reliance Global Services, Inc.; S4 Consultants, Inc.; Campbell & Spangler, Inc. and Spartan Solutions, Inc.;
Atty. RICHARD G. GARCIA of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester, LLP, Tel No. (415) 217-6990 appearing for Cross-Defendant Pro Unlimited, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PRO UNLIMITED INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Re Cross-Complaints By All Cross-Complainants is granted. First, Cross-Complainants' contentions that the existence of an agency and/or joint employment relationship give rise to an implied contractual obligation on the part of Cross-Defendant to pay Cross-Complainants directly under certain circumstances after having already making payments to Integriteam is neither supported by law nor the evidence presented. None of the facts here give rise to an inference that Cross-Defendant agreed to pay Cross-Complainants directly, particularly in the event that Cross-Defendant had already paid Integriteam. Second, Cross-Complainants cannot recover under a quantum meruit theory because Cross-Defendant has not been unjustly enriched, but instead has made payments for the services rendered by Cross-Complainants to Integriteam as contractually agreed by all parties. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS INT TECHNOLOGIES LLC, ITECH US INC., MERIDIAN TECHNOLOGIES INC., PALNAR, RELIANCE GLOBAL SERVICES, INC., S4 CONSULTANTS INC., SPARATAN SOLUTIONS and MEGANET SOLUTIONS LLC'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint IS Granted. Defendants shall file their proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The Court makes no determination as to whether the claims are permissive or compulsory because Defendants would be granted leave under either standard. Pursuant to CCP 426.50, a Court may only deny leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint if it finds the defendant is acting in bad faith. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) No showing of bad faith has been made here. If the claims are permissive, the Court finds that the substantially similar claims of Campbell & Spangler and Defendants should be tried in the same action. No trial date has yet been set. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PRO UNLIMITED INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Cross-Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. Cross-Complainant alleges that Integriteam, Inc. was acting as an agent for Cross-Defendant when Integriteam, Inc. entered into a consulting agreement with Cross-Complainant for consulting services for which Cross-Defendant Pro was the intended beneficiary. (FACC 5.) Cross-Defendant allegedly breached the consulting agreement when it failed to pay Cross-Complainant for consulting services rendered. (FACC 15.) Cross-Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts to state a cause of action for quantum meruit. Cross-Complainant is permitted to plead inconsistent legal theories. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
